title: What the Rattlesnake Said
content:
(Moon Poems for the Children/Fairy-tales for the Children)

         The moon's a little prairie-dog.
         He shivers through the night.
         He sits upon his hill and cries
         For fear that I will bite.

         The sun's a broncho.    He's afraid
         Like every other thing,
         And trembles, morning, noon and night,
         Lest I should spring, and sting.
meaning:
"What the Rattlesnake Said" is a quintessential example of Vachel Lindsay's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
